reasoning: An album holding the #1 spot for 16 consecutive weeks on the Billboard 200 is historically rare — no album has achieved 16 straight weeks since the Titanic soundtrack in 1998. Taylor Swift's previous album, The Tortured Poets Department, managed 12 consecutive weeks before dropping. 'The Life of a Showgirl' set records with 4M units in week 1, but experienced a massive 92% drop in pure sales in week 2, and by week 3 units were at 194,000. Maintaining a streak of 16 consecutive weeks requires staying #1 into late January, navigating through massive holiday music streams in December (where compilation and Christmas albums frequently take over the charts) and holding off all new major releases. Given the steep drop in units and historical precedent, the likelihood of avoiding any interruptions for 16 continuous weeks is exceptionally low.
